concept: Exit / Go Out / Leave a Space
description: To go out of or leave a place; the way out. 'Phiru' names the outward crossing—moving from inside to outside, departure from enclosed space.
sound_symbolism: The word moves out. 'Phi' /ɸi/ begins with breath releasing, departure starting. Then 'ru' rolls outward, exit continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb or noun: 'mi phiru' (I exit), 'lo phiru' (the exit). Opposite of 'koema' (enter).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Exiting is also beginning—leaving one space opens another, departure enables new arrival.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Exiting gracefully matters—how we leave shapes what we leave behind.
